messages.content:
Answer this medical question. Reply with only the letter.

OPV can be used if vaccine l monitor is showing?
(A) Colour of outer circle is same as inner square
(B) Colour of outer circle is darker than inner square
(C) Colour of outer circle is lighter than inner square
(D) None of the above
B: Colour of outer circle is darker than inner square

Answer this medical question. Reply with only the letter.

Average gain of height in first year ?
(A) 25 cms
(B) 50 cms
(C) 75 cms
(D) 100 cms
A: 25 cms

Answer this medical question. Reply with only the letter.

Investigation of choice to diagnose congenital malformations
(A) USG
(B) HSG
(C) MRI
(D) CT
C: MRI

Answer this medical question. Reply with only the letter.

Pain is appreciated when the small bowel is
(A) Burned
(B) crushed
(C) Cut
(D) distended
D: distended

Answer this medical question. Reply with only the letter.

The main advantage of RPD over FPD in replacing bilateral lost teeth is
(A) Cross arch stabilization
(B) Aesthetics
(C) Less expensive
(D) Comfort to the patient
A: Cross arch stabilization

Answer this medical question. Reply with only the letter.

Which is seen in chediak Higashi syndrome
(A) Neutopenia
(B) Defective microbial killing
(C) Presence of large granules in neutrophils
(D) All of the above
D: All of the above

Answer this medical question. Reply with only the letter.

The stye is other name of
(A) Chalazion
(B) Hordeolum internum
(C) Hordeolum externum
(D) None of the above
C: Hordeolum externum

Answer this medical question. Reply with only the letter.

Metal fume fever is common in chronic poisoning with -
(A) Mercury
(B) Lead
(C) Zinc
(D) Thallium
C: Zinc

Answer this medical question. Reply with only the letter.

Ionophores have following action except:
(A) Abolish proton gradient
(B) Inhibit ADP to ATP conversion
(C) Hydrophilic in character
(D) Abolish pH gradient
C: Hydrophilic in character

Answer this medical question. Reply with only the letter.

Which of the following part of psychosexual theory operates on the pleasure principle and attempts to obtain pleasure:
(A) Id
(B) Ego.
(C) Superego.
(D) None.
A: Id

Answer this medical question. Reply with only the letter.

Incidence of primary open-angle glaucoma in population over 40 years of age is about:
(A) 1 in 100
(B) 1 in 200
(C) 1 in 500
(D) 1 in 1000

Answer this medical question. Reply with only the letter.

Lysogenic conversion is seen in:
(A) C. diphtheriae
(B) Salmonella
(C) Staphylococcus
(D) E.coli
A: C. diphtheriae

Answer this medical question. Reply with only the letter.

Hot potato voice is characteristic of
(A) Pre tonsillar abscess
(B) Glottis carcinoma
(C) Subglottic carcinoma
(D) Supraglottic carcinoma
A: Pre tonsillar abscess

Answer this medical question. Reply with only the letter.

A child can Sit in Tripod position at which month?
(A) 5 months
(B) 6 months
(C) 8 months
(D) 9 months
B: 6 months

Answer this medical question. Reply with only the letter.

Serotonin syndrome may be precipitated by all of the following medications, except:
(A) Chlorpromazine
(B) Pentazocine
(C) Buspirone
(D) Meperidine
A: Chlorpromazine

Answer this medical question. Reply with only the letter.

Post prandial motility is maximum in
(A) Transverse colon
(B) Rectum
(C) Descending colon
(D) Sigmoid colon
D: Sigmoid colon

Answer this medical question. Reply with only the letter.

False statement regarding xenon is_____
(A) Rapid induction and emergence
(B) Free from greenhouse effects
(C) Teratogenic
(D) Does not trigger malignant hypehermia
C: Teratogenic

Answer this medical question. Reply with only the letter.

A Carrier of Neisseria Meningiditis can be detected by
(A) CSF - PCR
(B) CSF - Culture
(C) Blood culture
(D) Nasopharynegeal swab culture
D: Nasopharynegeal swab culture

Answer this medical question. Reply with only the letter.

Fibrosis is due to-
(A) TGF-b
(B) TNF-a
(C) IL - 7
(D) IL -10
A: TGF-b

Answer this medical question. Reply with only the letter.

Zero order kinetics means:
(A) A constant amount of drug is eliminated per unit time
(B) A constant fraction of the drug in the body is eliminated per unit time
(C) The fraction of the administered dose that reaches the systemic circulation.
(D) The effect that can be increased by giving a second agent that boosts the effect of the liver's enzyme system
A: A constant amount of drug is eliminated per unit time

Answer this medical question. Reply with only the letter.

A widely used drug that suppresses cellular immunity, inhibits prostaglandin and leukotriene synthesis and increases the catabolism of IgG antibody is:
(A) Cyclophosphamide
(B) Prednisone
(C) Cyclosporine
(D) Infliximab
B: Prednisone

Answer this medical question. Reply with only the letter.

Enzyme deficient in maple syrup urine disease -
(A) a-ketoacid decarboxylase
(B) Transaminase
(C) Isomerase
(D) Mutase
A: a-ketoacid decarboxylase

Answer this medical question. Reply with only the letter.

A chronic alcoholic patient is brought to the emergency department by his wife. The person has not consumed alcohol for the past two days due to religious reasons. The person complained of nausea, vomiting and dizziness. On the second day. he developed seizures, that progressed to generalized tonic clonic seizures (GTCS). Which of the following would be best medication to manage the seizure of the patient?
(A) Sodium valproate
(B) Phenytoin
(C) Diazepam
(D) Carbamazepine
C: Diazepam

Answer this medical question. Reply with only the letter.

Metronidazole is effective in all of the following conditions except -
(A) Pseudomembranous colitis
(B) Neurocysticercosis
(C) Giardiasis
(D) Amebic liver abscess
B: Neurocysticercosis

Answer this medical question. Reply with only the letter.

Which of the following is the causative agent of STD disease donovanosis?
(A) Hemophilus ducrevi
(B) Klebsiella granulomatis
(C) Leishmania donovani
(D) Treponema pallidum
B: Klebsiella granulomatis

Answer this medical question. Reply with only the letter.

A 55 years old female presents with tinnitus, dizziness and h/o progressive deafness. Differential diagnosis includes all except:
(A) Acoustic neuroma
(B) Endolymphatic hydrops
(C) Meningioma
(D) Histiocytosis-x
D: Histiocytosis-x

Answer this medical question. Reply with only the letter.

In primary tuberculosis, all of the following may be seen except:
(A) Cavitation
(B) Caseation
(C) Calcification
(D) Langhans giant cell
A: Cavitation

Answer this medical question. Reply with only the letter.

One of the following condition is not associated with clubbing :
(A) Primary biliary cirrhosis
(B) Chronic bronchitis
(C) Cryptogenic fibrosing alveolitis
(D) Central bronchiectasis
B: Chronic bronchitis

Answer this medical question. Reply with only the letter.

Conduction aphasia is classically seen due to lesion in -
(A) Arcuate fascicultis
(B) Cingulate gyms
(C) Bruges area
(D) Werniekes area
A: Arcuate fascicultis

Answer this medical question. Reply with only the letter.

Uveal Effusion syndrome may be associated with all of the following, Except:
(A) Myopia
(B) Ciliochoroidal detachment
(C) Structural defect in Sclera
(D) Nanophthalmos
A: Myopia

Answer this medical question. Reply with only the letter.

All of the following are Hepatotoxic except-
(A) Erythromycin
(B) Tetracycline
(C) Choloroquine
(D) Rifampicin
C: Choloroquine

Answer this medical question. Reply with only the letter.

Enophthalmos is seen in -
(A) Blow out fracture of orbit wall
(B) Hyperthyroidism
(C) Radiation Injuries
(D) Diabetes meilitus
A: Blow out fracture of orbit wall

Answer this medical question. Reply with only the letter.

Blue sclera is feature of -
(A) Osteogenesis imperfecta
(B) Osteopetrosis
(C) Cleidocranial dysostosis
(D) Achendroplasia
A: Osteogenesis imperfecta

Answer this medical question. Reply with only the letter.

Inducing agent of choice in DIC
(A) Thiopentone
(B) Ketamine
(C) Methohexitone
(D) Propofol
B: Ketamine

Answer this medical question. Reply with only the letter.

The classic triad of brucellosis include all, except
(A) Fever with profuse night sweats
(B) Meningoencephalitis
(C) Ahralgia
(D) Hepatosplenomegaly
B: Meningoencephalitis

Answer this medical question. Reply with only the letter.

The following vaccines can be given to immunocompromized child except?
(A) Recombinant
(B) Subunit
(C) Live attenuated
(D) Killed
C: Live attenuated

Answer this medical question. Reply with only the letter.

Which metabolite is involved in glycolysis, HMP shunt pathway as well as gluconeogenesis?
(A) Glucose-1-phosphate
(B) Glucose-6-phosphate
(C) Fructose-6-phosphate
(D) Pyruvate
B: Glucose-6-phosphate

Answer this medical question. Reply with only the letter.

The condition where glistening spots are found in tubal serosa:
(A) Walthard cell nests
(B) Metastases from ovary
(C) Paraovarian cysts
(D) Tubal fibrosis
C: Paraovarian cysts

Answer this medical question. Reply with only the letter.

IVF is done for infeility due to?
(A) Azoospermia
(B) Tubal infeility
(C) Infeility due to PID
(D) Uterine agenesis
A: Azoospermia

Answer this medical question. Reply with only the letter.

Ideal age for repair of vaginal agenesis is :
(A) 6 months
(B) 3 years
(C) At pubey
(D) Before marriage
D: Before marriage

Answer this medical question. Reply with only the letter.

Low grade squamous Intraepithelial leison of cervix in Bethseda system includes :
(A) CIN 1
(B) CIN 2
(C) CIN 3
(D) Squamous metaplasia
A: CIN 1

Answer this medical question. Reply with only the letter.

ALL of the following are true about trigeminal neuralgia
EXCEPT
(A) it is unilateral
(B) it is of throbbing nature
(C) it is triggered by touching cheeks, mucosa etc
(D) occurs in bouts
B: it is of throbbing nature

Answer this medical question. Reply with only the letter.

Keratin of skin and nail differ because of
(A) Disulphide bond
(B) Covalent bond
(C) Vander Waal bond
(D) Hydrogen bond
A: Disulphide bond

Answer this medical question. Reply with only the letter.

Which of the following are included in Malaria Vector Indices in pre-eradication era ? a)Spleen ratio b)Human blood index c) Slide positivity rate d)Parasite density index
(A) 1,3
(B) 1,4
(C) 1, 2, 3
(D) 4,3,1

Answer this medical question. Reply with only the letter.

In camera trial of a rape case hearing is done under ?
(A) 376 IPC
(B) 327 CrPC
(C) 53 CrPC
(D) 375 IPC
B: 327 CrPC

Answer this medical question. Reply with only the letter.

Which of the following anti-epileptic agents causes the reversible side-effect of gingival hyperplasia?
(A) Ethosuximide
(B) Phenobarbitone
(C) Sodium valproate
(D) None of the above
D: None of the above

Answer this medical question. Reply with only the letter.

Amyloid is best identified by -
(A) Staining with methyl violet
(B) Secondary fluorescence in UV light with riboflavin
(C) Congo red
(D) Green birefringence of stained amyloid when viewed by polarizing microscope
D: Green birefringence of stained amyloid when viewed by polarizing microscope

Answer this medical question. Reply with only the letter.

The red velvety appearance of stomach mucosa is seen in the poisoning of
(A) Lead
(B) Arsenic
(C) Copper
(D) Mercury
B: Arsenic

Answer this medical question. Reply with only the letter.

Maximum energy store in
(A) Adipose
(B) Liver
(C) Muscle
(D) Blood glucose
A: Adipose

Answer this medical question. Reply with only the letter.

60 year old diabetic female presented with burning sensation to spicy food. Intraoral examination revealed multiple periodontal abscess and keratotic area in a lace pattern with occasional erosive areas inside the lace pattern.

Provisional diagnosis for this lady will be
(A) Oral hairy Leukoplakia
(B) Oral lichen planus
(C) Oral squamous cell carcinoma
(D) Oral pemphigus
B: Oral lichen planus

Answer this medical question. Reply with only the letter.

Which of the following is selective alpha antagonist?
(A) Propanolol
(B) Prazosin
(C) Phentolamine
(D) Clonidine
B: Prazosin

Answer this medical question. Reply with only the letter.

A 12 year old girl with the mood and emotional liability has a golden brown discoloration in descement membrane. Most likely diagnosis is ?
(A) Fabry's disease
(B) Wilson's disease
(C) Glycogen storage disease
(D) Acute rheumatic fever
B: Wilson's disease

Answer this medical question. Reply with only the letter.

A patient with mild skin pigmentation comes to you because of sudden abdominal pain, fever and a rigid abdomen. Her lab repo show blood sugar of 55, Na 119, and K 6.2. Her BP 88/58. She undergoes exploratory laparotomy. Which statement is true?
(A) Treatment with exogenous steroids is usually ineffective
(B) This condition is commonly seen as a consequence of metastasis of distant cancers, such as lung or breast to the adrenal glands
(C) Death from untreated chronic adrenal insufficiency may occur within hours of surgery
(D) The most common underlying cause today is infection with MDR tuberculosis
C: Death from untreated chronic adrenal insufficiency may occur within hours of surgery

Answer this medical question. Reply with only the letter.

Normal safe limit of Fluoride content in drinking water is
(A) 0.5 to 0.8 mg/L
(B) 0.8 to 1.0 mg/L
(C) 0.2 to 0.5 mg/L
(D) 0.2 to 0.8 mg/L
A: 0.5 to 0.8 mg/L

Answer this medical question. Reply with only the letter.

Which of the following is not a point mutation-
(A) Silent mutation
(B) Nonsense mutation
(C) Frame shift mutation
(D) Mis-sense mutation
C: Frame shift mutation

Answer this medical question. Reply with only the letter.

Test used for cholesterol is
(A) Salkowski test
(B) Schilling test
(C) Zellwenger’s test
(D) Brodie’s test
A: Salkowski test

Answer this medical question. Reply with only the letter.

The light brown perinuclear pigment seen on H & Estaining of the cardiac muscle fibres in the grosslynormal appearing hea of an 83 year old man atautopsy is due to deposition os:
(A) Hemosiderin
(B) Lipochrome
(C) Cholesterol metabolite
(D) Anthracotic pigment
B: Lipochrome

Answer this medical question. Reply with only the letter.

Apparant volume of distribution of a drug less than 5 liters implies that the drug is predominantly in which of the following compament?
(A) Extracellular fluid
(B) Total body water
(C) Intracellular fluid
(D) Intravascular fluid
A: Extracellular fluid

Answer this medical question. Reply with only the letter.

This is a paramyxovirus and causes the syndrome known as croup.
(A) Measles virus
(B) Influenza virus
(C) Respiratory syncytial virus
(D) Parainfluenza virus
D: Parainfluenza virus

Answer this medical question. Reply with only the letter.

In emergency tracheostomy the following structures are damaged except?
(A) Isthmus of the thyroid
(B) Inferior thyroid aery
(C) Thyroid ima
(D) Inferior thyroid vein
B: Inferior thyroid aery

Answer this medical question. Reply with only the letter.

The	primary	excretory	organ	for	the	local
anaesthetic and its metabolites is:
(A) Lungs
(B) Kidneys
(C) Rectum
(D) Skin
B: Kidneys

Answer this medical question. Reply with only the letter.

A case of murder with gunshot is reported. A metal bullet is recovered from the body. Primary and Secondary markings on a metal bullet can be used for
(A) Identification of weapon
(B) To know the range of firing
(C) Severity of tissue damage
(D) To know time of crime
A: Identification of weapon

Answer this medical question. Reply with only the letter.

A neonate is suspected to be suffering from necrotizing entercolitis (NEC). On fuhur examination and investigation, he is diagnosed to be Bell&;s stage INEC. The management of choice would be
(A) Laporotomy and proceed
(B) Inseion of bilateral pelvic drains
(C) Conservative management with IV fluids and antibiotics
(D) Initial conservative management and laparotomy after 24 hours
C: Conservative management with IV fluids and antibiotics

Answer this medical question. Reply with only the letter.

False statement regarding this condition:
(A) Associated with HLA DR4, DR5, DQ3
(B) Exclamation mark seen
(C) Spares grey and white hairs
(D) Itching and scaling seen
D: Itching and scaling seen

Answer this medical question. Reply with only the letter.

An intrauterine pregnancy of approximately 10 weeks gestation is confirmed in a 30-year-old gravida 5, para 4 woman with an IUD in place. The patient expresses a strong desire for the pregnancy to be continued. On examination, the string of the IUD is noted to be protruding from the cervical os. The most appropriate course of action is to
(A) Leave the IUD in place without any other treatment
(B) Leave the IUD in place and continue prophylactic antibiotics throughout pregnancy
(C) Remove the IUD immediately
(D) Terminate the pregnancy because of the high risk of infection
C: Remove the IUD immediately

Answer this medical question. Reply with only the letter.

CHF in an infant is characterized by all Except
(A) Pedal edema
(B) Tachypnea
(C) Sweating
(D) Poor weight gain
A: Pedal edema

Answer this medical question. Reply with only the letter.

Reverse transcriptase polymerase chain reactions can aid in diagnosis of all of the following viral infections except -
(A) Adenovirus
(B) Astrovirus
(C) Rotavirus
(D) Poliovirus
A: Adenovirus

Answer this medical question. Reply with only the letter.

Classical indications of gamma knife amongst the following is:
(A) Glioblastoma
(B) Arterio-venous malformation
(C) Tuberculoma
(D) Hepatocellular carcinoma
B: Arterio-venous malformation

Answer this medical question. Reply with only the letter.

Translation occurs in :
(A) Ribosomes
(B) Mitochondria
(C) Nucleus
(D) Cytoplasm
A: Ribosomes

Answer this medical question. Reply with only the letter.

Vocal cords are supplied by?
(A) External laryngeal nerve
(B) Internal laryngeal nerve
(C) Recurrent laryngeal nerve
(D) Vagus nerve
C: Recurrent laryngeal nerve

Answer this medical question. Reply with only the letter.

Treatment of stage III carcinoma ovary: March 2013
(A) Total hysterectomy
(B) Total hysterectomy + bilateral salpingo-oophorectomy
(C) Debulking surgery + removal of tumour
(D) None of the above
C: Debulking surgery + removal of tumour

Answer this medical question. Reply with only the letter.

Frecieuncy of Rest tremor in Parkinson's disease is -
(A) 2/sec
(B) 14/sec
(C) 4-6/sec
(D) 8/sec
C: 4-6/sec

Answer this medical question. Reply with only the letter.

A 59 yr old man with severe myxomatous mitral regurgitation is asymptomatic,wi wia left ventricular ejection fraction of 45% and an endsystollic diameter index of 2.9cm/m2 . The most appropriate treatment is
(A) Mitral valve repair or replacement
(B) No treatment
(C) ACE inhibitor therapy
(D) Digoxin and diuretic therapy
A: Mitral valve repair or replacement

Answer this medical question. Reply with only the letter.

The marked structure on the X-ray is:
(A) Nasopharynx
(B) Ethmoidal air cells
(C) Sphenoid sinus
(D) Pituitary gland
C: Sphenoid sinus

Answer this medical question. Reply with only the letter.

Ampicillin prophylaxis is given in -
(A) Rectal surgery
(B) Splenectomy
(C) Head and neck surgery
(D) Biliary surgery
A: Rectal surgery

Answer this medical question. Reply with only the letter.

Acetyl coA can be directly converted to all except
(A) Glucose
(B) Fatty acids
(C) Cholesterol
(D) Ketone bodies
A: Glucose

Answer this medical question. Reply with only the letter.

On mammogram all of the following are the features of a malignant tumor except
(A) Spiculation
(B) Microcalcification
(C) Macrocalcification
(D) Irregular mass
C: Macrocalcification

Answer this medical question. Reply with only the letter.

Drugs used for treating schizophrenia are chiefly: March 2010
(A) Antimaniac
(B) Antidepressant
(C) Antihistaminics
(D) Antipsychotic
D: Antipsychotic

Answer this medical question. Reply with only the letter.

Codex ali Men tarius is related to -
(A) Water purity
(B) Milk purity
(C) Air pollution
(D) Food stardardization
D: Food stardardization

Answer this medical question. Reply with only the letter.

Most specific symptom of temporal aeritis?
(A) Visual loss
(B) Jaw claudication
(C) Temporal headache
(D) Polymyalgia rheumatica
B: Jaw claudication

Answer this medical question. Reply with only the letter.

Patient with pelvic fracture presents with anuria. After 24 hrs bladder is not palpable. Possible diagnosis includes:a) Bulbar urethral injuryb) Posterior urethral injuryc) Extraperitoneal rupture of bladderd) Intraperitoneal rupture of bladder
(A) ab
(B) bc
(C) bd
(D) cd
D: cd

Answer this medical question. Reply with only the letter.

All of the following are true about anopheles type of mosquito Except –
(A) Eggs are boat shaped and provided with lateral floats
(B) Larvae rest parallel to water surface
(C) Larvae have siphon tube
(D) Pupae have broad and short siphon
C: Larvae have siphon tube

Answer this medical question. Reply with only the letter.

The following hemotoxylin and eosin stained biopsy is from which tissue?
(A) Tonsils
(B) Spleen
(C) Lymph Node
(D) Peyer's patches
A: Tonsils

Answer this medical question. Reply with only the letter.

Which is most common site of Ca pancreas?
(A) Head
(B) Body
(C) Tail
(D) Neck
A: Head

Answer this medical question. Reply with only the letter.

The following are function of restriction endonuclease, EXCEPT:
(A) Cut both the strands of double stranded DNA
(B) The cut ends produced by restriction endonuclease are sticky
(C) The cut ends produced by restriction endonuclease are blunt
(D) It is non specific to base pairs
D: It is non specific to base pairs

Answer this medical question. Reply with only the letter.

Which ovarian tumor is likely to involve the opposite ovary by metastasis :
(A) Granulosa cell tumor
(B) Dysgerminoma
(C) Gynandroblastoma
(D) Endodermal sinus tumor
A: Granulosa cell tumor

Answer this medical question. Reply with only the letter.

Totipotency of embryonic stem cell is due to that they?
(A) Can differentiate into all the tissues of embryonic or extraembryonic cell types.
(B) Can differentiate into cells derived from any of the three germ layers.
(C) Can differentiate only into cells of a closely related family
(D) Can differentiate only in to one cell type
A: Can differentiate into all the tissues of embryonic or extraembryonic cell types.

Answer this medical question. Reply with only the letter.

A known case of chronic kidney disease stage 4 is on insulin therapy. What adjustment in insulin dosage is required?
(A) Increase dose of insulin
(B) Decrease dose of insulin
(C) Variable
(D) No alteration in dose
B: Decrease dose of insulin

Answer this medical question. Reply with only the letter.

All of the following drugs can result in amorphous whorl like corneal opacities except:
(A) Amiodarone
(B) Chloroquine
(C) Indomethacin
(D) Chlorpromazine
D: Chlorpromazine

Answer this medical question. Reply with only the letter.

In gram-negative septicemia, early findings before shock sets in is -
(A) ↑ Cardiac output,↑ Total peripheral resistance
(B) ↑ Cardiac output, ↓ Total peripheral resistance
(C) ↓Cardiac output, ↑ Total peripheral resistance
(D) ↓ Cardiac output, ↓ Total peripheral resistance
B: ↑ Cardiac output, ↓ Total peripheral resistance

Answer this medical question. Reply with only the letter.

The most common malignancy that produces ichthyosis?
(A) Hodgkin's disease
(B) Mycosis fungoides
(C) Kaposi's sarcoma
(D) Carcinoma breast
A: Hodgkin's disease

Answer this medical question. Reply with only the letter.

4 months old infant had respiratory distress.It was diagnosed as bronchiolitis. most common cause of this is
(A) Measles
(B) Respiratory syncytial virus
(C) Influenza
(D) Para Influenza virus
B: Respiratory syncytial virus

Answer this medical question. Reply with only the letter.

Suprabony pockets are also known as
(A) Infrabony
(B) Intra-alveolar
(C) Subcrestal
(D) Supracrestal/Supra-alveolar
D: Supracrestal/Supra-alveolar

Answer this medical question. Reply with only the letter.

Which of the following is not a phospholipid
(A) Sphingomyelin
(B) Cephalin
(C) Cerebroside
(D) Cardiolipin
C: Cerebroside

Answer this medical question. Reply with only the letter.

Modified radical mastoidectomy is indicated in all except -
(A) Safe CSOM
(B) Unsafe CSOM with attticoantra! disease
(C) Coalescent mastoiditis
(D) Limited mastoid pathology
A: Safe CSOM

Answer this medical question. Reply with only the letter.

Most stable amino acid at physiological pH is
(A) Histidine
(B) Lysine
(C) Arginine
(D) Glycine
A: Histidine

Answer this medical question. Reply with only the letter.

Cyclosporine is used in the management of -
(A) Disciform Keratitis
(B) Anterior Iridocyclitis
(C) Rhegmatogenous retinal detachment
(D) Phacomorphic glaucoma
A: Disciform Keratitis

Answer this medical question. Reply with only the letter.

A 3 week old child presented to the pediatrician with meningitis. A presumptive diagnosis of late onset perinatal meningitis was made. The CSF culture was positive for gram positive bacilli. Which of the following characteristic of this bacteria would be helpful in differentiating it from other bacterial agents:-
(A) Ability to grow on blood agar
(B) Ability to produce catalase
(C) Fermentative attack on sugars
(D) Motility at 25 degree Celsius.
D: Motility at 25 degree Celsius.

Answer this medical question. Reply with only the letter.

Most vascular structure of eyeball is
(A) Iris
(B) Ciliary body
(C) Choroid
(D) Sclera
C: Choroid

Answer this medical question. Reply with only the letter.

Vitamin not deficient in celiac disease is?
(A) Vitamin D
(B) Vitamin B 12
(C) Folic acid
(D) Vitamin A
C: Folic acid

Answer this medical question. Reply with only the letter.

Which of the positions best describes the location of celiac plexus
(A) Anterior or anterolateral to Aoa
(B) Posterolateral to Aoa
(C) Anterolateral to sympathetic chain
(D) Anteromedial to sympathetic chain
A: Anterior or anterolateral to Aoa

Answer this medical question. Reply with only the letter.

Drugs highly bound to albumin
(A) NSAID'S
(B) Lidocaine
(C) Beta blockers
(D) Verapamil
A: NSAID'S

Answer this medical question. Reply with only the letter.

Adverse effect of phenytoin include the following except-
(A) Lymphadenopathy
(B) Ataxia
(C) Hypercalcemia
(D) Hirsutism
C: Hypercalcemia

Answer this medical question. Reply with only the letter.

Breast milk storage in a refrigerator is upto ?
(A) 4 hrs
(B) 8 hrs
(C) 12 hours
(D) 24 hrs
D: 24 hrs

Answer this medical question. Reply with only the letter.

Which of the following signs is not suggestive of a cervical spinal cord injury :
(A) Flaccidity
